subject: Anthropology
title: Trauma and Resilience
description: Learn Trauma and Resilience in one minute.

hook.text: PTSD was defined by Western wars. But does the same diagnosis fit a Rwandan genocide survivor or a Syrian refugee?

buildup.text: Trauma responses vary across cultures. How people process violence and loss depends on local meaning systems.

discovery.text: Some cultures heal trauma through collective ritual, not individual therapy. Community ceremonies restore social bonds.

twist.text: Exporting PTSD as a universal label can pathologize normal grief responses and undermine local healing practices.

climax.text: Trauma is real, but resilience is cultural. How communities heal depends on shared meaning, not just clinical treatment.

punchline.text: Healing from trauma is a community act, not just a clinical one.

quiz.question: How do some cultures heal trauma?
options: Through collective rituals that restore social bonds
Trauma doesn't exist outside the West
